BankAccount.title: BankAccount
BankAccount.description: Represents a bank account owned by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the bank account.

accountName.description: The name of the bank account.

balance.description: The current balance of the bank account.

currency.description: The currency of the bank account.

transactionDate.description: The date and time of the transaction.

transactionType.description: The type of transaction (e.g., debit, credit).

category.description: The category of the transaction (e.g., food, shopping).

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the application.

Beneficiary.title: Beneficiary
Beneficiary.description: Represents a beneficiary to whom the user can send money.

ScheduledPayment.title: ScheduledPayment
ScheduledPayment.description: Represents a future-dated or recurring payment.

definition.description: Stores user profiles.  Access is restricted to the user themselves.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores bank accounts owned by the user. Enables atomic operations and simplifies security rules based on path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: bankAccountId
params.description: The unique identifier of the bank account.

definition.description: Stores transactions associated with a specific bank account owned by the user.  Enables atomic operations and simplifies security rules based on path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: bankAccountId
params.description: The unique identifier of the bank account.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the transaction.

definition.description: Stores beneficiaries associated with the user.  Enables atomic operations and simplifies security rules based on path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

params.name: beneficiaryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the beneficiary.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure authorization independence, clarity, and scalability. It leverages path-based ownership for user-related data and segregates data based on access requirements.

1.  **Users Collection:** Stores user profiles. Access is restricted to the user themselves.
2.  **BankAccounts Collection:** Bank accounts are stored as a subcollection of the user document (`/users/{userId}/bankAccounts/{bankAccountId}`). This enforces ownership and simplifies security rules.  Authorization Independence is maintained because access control is determined solely by the path and the `userId` derived from `request.auth.uid`.  No `get()` calls are needed to check ownership.
3.  **Transactions Collection:** Transactions are stored as a subcollection of the bank account (`/users/{userId}/bankAccounts/{bankAccountId}/transactions/{transactionId}`).  Similar to bank accounts, this structure provides strong ownership and simplifies security rules. Authorization Independence is maintained as access is determined by the nested path and the `userId` which implicitly authorizes the user. No `get()` calls needed.
4.  **Beneficiaries Collection:** Beneficiaries are stored as a subcollection of the user document (`/users/{userId}/beneficiaries/{beneficiaryId}`). This enforces ownership and simplifies security rules.  Authorization Independence is maintained because access control is determined solely by the path and the `userId` derived from `request.auth.uid`.  No `get()` calls are needed to check ownership.
5. **ScheduledPayments Collection:** Stores future-dated payments for a user, maintaining the same ownership pattern for security.

**Authorization Independence:** This is achieved by nesting BankAccounts, Transactions, and Beneficiaries under the /users/{userId} path. Security rules can then rely solely on `request.auth.uid == userId` to grant access, without needing to read parent documents.

**Query Access Patterns (QAPs):**

*   **List BankAccounts for User:**  Securely list bank accounts using `/users/{userId}/bankAccounts`. Rules will check `request.auth.uid == userId`.
*   **List Transactions for BankAccount:** Securely list transactions using `/users/{userId}/bankAccounts/{bankAccountId}/transactions`. Rules will check `request.auth.uid == userId`.
*   **List Beneficiaries for User:** Securely list beneficiaries using `/users/{userId}/beneficiaries`. Rules will check `request.auth.uid == userId`.

The design adheres to structural segregation by storing private user data (bank accounts, transactions, beneficiaries) under user-specific paths, ensuring uniform security requirements within each collection.
